cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is asking which option is most similar to O'Keeffe's relationship with nature. The passage gives a lot of information about her life and art. Let me read through the passage again to make sure I understand her relationship with nature. So, Georgia O'Keeffe was born in Wisconsin and grew up on a farm. When she was 17, she wanted to be an artist and left for schools in Chicago and New York, but she never lost her bond with the land. She painted the things that were most important to her, which were simplified portrayals of nature. She became famous when her works were exhibited by Stieglitz, whom she married. After visiting New Mexico in 1929, she was moved by the Western desert's landscape and started painting its images, including cows' skulls and bones. After her husband's death, she moved there permanently and used the desert's elements as subjects. She continued making art about the Western desert until her death in 1986. The passage also mentions she's considered a pioneering American modernist, with her own vision, viewing painting as a private endeavor, and her work being more distinctly American in its treatment of natural forms.
